faqs.question: What is psoriasis?
faqs.answer: Psoriasis is a chronic autoimmune skin condition that causes red, scaly patches due to accelerated skin cell growth. It often follows a relapsing-remitting course and can be associated with joint involvement (psoriatic arthritis).

faqs.question: What triggers psoriasis flares?
faqs.answer: Common triggers include stress, skin injury, infections (e.g., streptococcal), certain medications, cold/dry weather, smoking, and excessive alcohol. Managing triggers and good skin care helps reduce flares.

faqs.question: How is psoriasis treated?
faqs.answer: Treatment depends on severity and location: topical therapies (corticosteroids, vitamin D analogues), phototherapy (UVB), systemic agents (methotrexate, cyclosporine), and biologics for moderate-to-severe disease. Work with a dermatologist to choose the right plan.

faqs.question: Can lifestyle changes help psoriasis?
faqs.answer: Yes. Weight management, smoking cessation, limiting alcohol, stress reduction, and regular moisturizing can improve outcomes and reduce flare frequency.
